title: Fix watcher-daemon initialization race conditions
description: Investigate and implement a robust auto-recovery mechanism in watcher-daemon's startup sequence. The daemon should mirror the bd CLI's resilience by actively killing orphaned Dolt servers or retrying connections when 'OpenFromConfig' hits a circuit breaker, rather than instantly exiting and relying on the user to manually run 'bd doctor'.

title: Fix Daemon Connection Leak
description: Refactor AppState to maintain a single persistent BeadsService instance per project instead of spinning up new daemon processes on every refresh tick, which causes 'too many open files' OS errors.

title: Fix + expand the Issue Inspector dependency section
description:
In lib/widgets/issue_inspector.dart (_buildDependenciesSection, ~161-217):
1. FIX INVERTED LABELS (verified bug): issue.dependencies.where(type=='blocks').map(dependsOnId) is currently labeled 'Blocks' but per canonical bd direction these are what the issue is 'Blocked By'. The reverse-lookup list is the inverse. Swap the labels so they match bd blocked.
2. Add a Hierarchy section: clickable Parent and Children (reuse IssueHierarchy).
3. Render 'related' and 'discovered-from' dependencies ('Discovered from ->' provenance).
4. Show each blocker's live status (dim/strike closed blockers) so it's clear which are still active.
Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: Model foundation: readiness + correct blocks direction
description:
Add an IssueDependencies extension in lib/models/issue.dart (sibling to IssueHierarchy), computed against the in-memory issue list:
- List<Issue> blockers(all): open issues blocking this one = this issue's own deps of type 'blocks' whose target issue is NOT closed.
- List<Issue> blocking(all): reverse lookup = issues whose 'blocks' dep depends_on_id == this.id.
- bool isBlocked => blockers(all).isNotEmpty.

CANONICAL DIRECTION (verified against bd blocked in read-aloud): a dependency {depends_on_id: Y, type: 'blocks'} stored on issue X means 'X is blocked by Y'. ijo.3 carries depends_on=89j.3 and bd reports 'ijo.3 Blocked by [89j.3]'.

Add unit tests locking this direction using that exact example. This is the keystone for all UI work in this epic.

title: Epic: Dependency & Readiness Visualization
description: Watcher renders the parent-child tree fully but the 'blocks' DAG (the layer bd ready/bd blocked run on) is nearly invisible. On tree/kanban/dashboard a blocked issue looks identical to an actionable one. This epic surfaces readiness everywhere, fixes a verified blocks-direction bug in the inspector, renders all dependency types, and adds dependency-centric views. See docs/DEPENDENCY_VISUALIZATION_PROPOSAL.md.

title: Remove dead legacy Gemini single-model config path
description: Delete the orphaned legacy single-model config: geminiModel, vertexLocation, setGeminiModel(), setVertexLocation() in app_state.dart (never called/read), and the unused _vertexLocationController in settings_modal.dart (initialized/disposed but never wired to a widget). The active path is the aiModels list + defaultAiModel used by GenerativeAiService.
close_reason: Removed all legacy single-model config properties (geminiModel, vertexLocation, setGeminiModel, setVertexLocation) in app_state.dart and deleted the unused _vertexLocationController in settings_modal.dart

title: Epic: AI Model Config Cleanup & Terminal Integration Hardening
description: Remove the dead legacy single-model Gemini config path, refresh default model seeds with proper migration, and harden the Ghostty/AppleScript terminal orchestration bridge (escaping, preflight checks, readiness polling, libghostty spike).

title: CI: add analyze/test/lint workflow
description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to run flutter analyze, flutter test, and go test on every PR/push.
close_reason: Created .github/workflows/ci.yml and formatted all Dart files to pass the formatting check.

title: Add unit tests for IssueHierarchy and BeadsService RPC
description: Implement robust unit tests for pure hierarchy/tree logic and mock BeadsService JSON-RPC stdin/stdout interactions.
close_reason: Added comprehensive unit test suite in test/models/issue_test.dart covering all behaviors of the IssueHierarchy extension

title: Epic: Watcher Code Health and Testability Improvements
description: Establish robust quality gates, split the massive AppState class, decouple the background services from the UI singleton, and implement unit tests.
close_reason: All 5 child subtasks completed successfully. Closing epic.

title: Refactor and add unit tests to Go daemon
description:
1. Added package-level outputWriter and refactored RPC dispatching to dispatchRequest function in main.go for better testability.
2. Implemented main_test.go to test ping and graph methods.
3. Appended PATH env to killCmd in main.go to prevent execution failures in macOS GUI environments.
4. Resolved a couple of Dart static analysis and deprecation warnings.
close_reason: Completed all refactoring, Go daemon tests, subprocess environments, and static analysis cleaning.

title: Fix column depends_on_id exception
description: Upgrade beads library dependency to v1.0.5 to resolve 'depends_on_id' column not found error when selecting projects (like abra-go).
close_reason: Upgraded github.com/steveyegge/beads to v1.0.5 and verified graph loading query against abra-go project. Recompiled watcher-daemon and re-installed app.

title: Add Draggable and DragTarget to TreeNode
description: Wrap the tree node row in Flutter's Draggable/DragTarget widgets to allow changing task parents.
close_reason: Added Draggable and DragTarget to TreeNode

title: Support parent updates in Dart layer
description: Update AppState and BeadsService to accept an optional parent issue ID in updateIssue RPC calls.

title: Drag and Drop Tasks in Tree View
description: Allow dragging tasks and dropping them onto epics to change their parent dependency.

title: Dynamic icu4c brew path
description: Update Makefile and README to dynamically locate icu4c via brew --prefix

title: Create task - Container UI
description:
When this fix for watcher-56r was done,  the contrast is now way off and the background  is darker 
The  picklists work well though
close_reason: Updated MacosSheet modals to use MacosTheme.of(context).canvasColor instead of windowBackgroundColor to fix contrast and transparency issues

title: Verify Interaction and Comment Dart Parsing
description: Ensure the Dart models (Interaction and Comment parsing logic) gracefully handle string UUIDs instead of integers for the 'id' field to align with the beads v0.61.0 schema migration.

title: Bump Daemon Dependency to v0.61.0
description: Update daemon/go.mod to pull in github.com/steveyegge/beads@v0.61.0 to inherit the new UUID database schema and circuit breaker logic. Recompile the daemon.

title: Implement Native Reordering in macos_ui Fork
description: Switch context to the macos_ui fork (~/projects/github/macos_ui) and implement Option 2 from our feature request: Native Reorderable Support. This requires deep structural changes to lib/src/layout/sidebar/sidebar_items.dart to integrate Flutter's ReorderableListView while perfectly preserving the complex MacosTheme color resolution and private _SidebarItem layout constraints.
close_reason: Abandoned: Replaced by Settings screen workaround due to macos_ui library constraints.

title: Implement Issue Comments in Inspector
description:
1. Add 'get_comments' and 'add_comment' JSON-RPC methods to watcher-daemon.
2. Wire these methods through BeadsService and AppState.
3. Update the IssueInspector widget to display a timeline of comments for the selected issue.
4. Add a multiline MacosTextField and 'Add Note' button to the bottom of the inspector to submit new comments, attributed to the configured user identity.

title: Implement JSON-RPC Heartbeat for Daemon Boot
description: Update the Go daemon to emit a 'booting' or 'heartbeat' JSON-RPC message while it waits for the underlying Dolt database server to initialize. Update the Dart BeadsService to listen for this message to reset its timeout, preventing slow-booting databases from triggering artificial 'Daemon Crashed' exceptions in the UI.

title: Fix JSON-RPC parsing for massive payloads
description: Replace the string accumulator buffer in BeadsService with Dart's streaming json.decoder to properly handle massive JSON-RPC responses (like the 40k+ char graph from best-practices) without memory fragmentation or parsing errors.

title: Fix JSON-RPC Stream Truncation
description: Replace Dart's default LineSplitter in the daemon stdout listener with a robust JSON stream parser (or chunk accumulator) to prevent massive payloads (like the best-practices graph) from being silently dropped due to buffer size limits, resulting in false 'exit code 0' crashes.

title: Fix Modal Visual Artifacts
description: Wrap the contents of MacosSheet modals (like FederationModal) in a MacosScaffold or explicit Container with MacosColors.controlBackgroundColor to prevent underlying UI elements from bleeding through translucently.

title: Fix Daemon Graph Endpoint Dependencies
description: Update the watcher-daemon handleGraph method to manually query and attach the dependencies array to the returned JSON, which is missing from the base types.Issue struct.

title: Fix implicit parent-child dependency parsing
description: Ensure that the Tree View correctly infers parent-child relationships from dot-notation in issue IDs (like how bd list --tree does) so that 4fi.1 renders as a child of 4fi

title: macOS HIG Compliance for Tree View
description: Update the Tree View rendering to strictly align with Apple's macOS Human Interface Guidelines for Outline Views

title: New view: Ready Queue (mirror of bd ready)
description: Add a 'Ready' view (segmented control alongside Dashboard/Kanban/Tree, registered in router.dart + home_screen sidebar). A flat, priority-sorted list of ONLY actionable issues: status open/in_progress, not isBlocked, and (configurable) no open children. This is the single most useful missing view -- it answers 'what should I work on now' that the tree/kanban cannot. Clicking an item opens the inspector. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: New view: Blocked / Impediments (mirror of bd blocked)
description: Add a 'Blocked' view listing every blocked issue with its open blockers inline (id, title, status), grouped or sorted by priority -- the triage counterpart to the Ready Queue. Each blocker is a clickable link to navigate. Mirrors 'bd blocked' output. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: Dashboard: Ready and Blocked stat cards
description: In lib/screens/project_dashboard.dart add 'Ready' and 'Blocked' counts alongside the existing status/priority stat cards. Ready = open issues that are not blocked and (optionally) have no open children; Blocked = isBlocked. This is the at-a-glance 'what is actionable now' number mirroring bd ready / bd blocked. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: Surface dependency counts on cards/rows
description: dependencyCount and dependentCount are parsed in issue.dart but never displayed. Surface them (or the locally computed blockers/blocking counts) as small affordances on kanban cards and tree rows, e.g. 'down2 up1', so high-leverage hub issues are visible. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: Readiness badges on Kanban cards and Tree rows
description:
Consume isBlocked/blockers from the model foundation:
- kanban_card.dart: add a red 'Blocked by N' badge below the type badge when isBlocked; optionally dim blocked cards. Leave ready cards clean.
- tree_node.dart (_buildIssueRow): add a small blocker pip/count on blocked rows, DISTINCT from the existing status=='blocked' badge (which only reflects the literal status string, not computed blockage).
Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: Harden terminal AppleScript orchestration
description: In tmux_service.dart: escape/validate session names interpolated into AppleScript for Ghostty/iTerm2/Terminal (custom names via setProjectTmuxSessionName are NOT sanitized today, unlike effectiveTmuxSessionName); add a preflight check that Ghostty.app is installed with a friendly error (parity with tmux fallback); replace the racy fixed 500ms launch delay with window-readiness polling/retries.
close_reason: Hardened AppleScript terminal orchestration: Sanitized custom tmux session names, implemented POSIX preflight application checks for Ghostty and iTerm2 via osascript, and replaced the racy fixed 500ms launch delay with structured window-readiness AppleScript polling.

title: Refresh default Gemini model seed + add versioned migration
description: Update the first-run aiModels seed (app_state.dart ~192-205) away from fragile -preview identifiers to the current latest flash model (confirm exact identifier + region against the live Vertex catalog). Add a versioned migration so existing installs (which already have saved prefs and skip the empty-list seed) also pick up the new default. Fix the stale add_model_modal.dart placeholder (currently gemini-2.0-flash-001).
close_reason: Updated seed to gemini-3.5-flash@global (live-tested 200 OK), added versioned migration (model_seed_version=2) that upgrades existing installs in-place, fixed add_model_modal placeholder, added 3 unit tests covering seed/migration/idempotency.

title: Unify issue-mutation path and remove dead daemon handlers
description: Ensure issue creation and updates use the Go daemon RPC client instead of shell execution, and clean up unused handlers.
close_reason: Migrated createIssue to use the daemon RPC create_issue with auto-dependency insertion, removed unused handleCloseIssue daemon handler, and formatted all files.

title: Extract shared dev-PATH constant (Dart & Go)
description: Deduplicate the PATH environment string literal in Dart and Go to make dev environments easily configurable.
close_reason: Extracted shared dev-PATH constant to macosDefaultPath and macosPathEnv in beads_service.dart and macosDeveloperPath in main.go

title: Exclude build/** from analyzer and remove debug.json
description: Fix the unscoped analyzer issues by excluding build/**, regenerate the json_serializable code, and delete the committed debug.json artifact.
close_reason: Excluded build/** from analyzer and removed debug.json

title: UI: Diagnostic Results Modal
description: Implement a beautiful macOS-native modal to display health check results with actionable fixes.
close_reason: Implemented health check action button and diagnostic results modal.

title: UI: Project Health Action Button
description: Add a 'Run Health Check' button to the Project Dashboard ToolBar.
close_reason: Implemented health check action button and diagnostic results modal.

title: Service: Implement HealthService in Dart
description: Create a new service or update BeadsService to call check_health and parse the diagnostic payloads.
close_reason: Implemented HealthCheck models, BeadsService method, and exposed it via AppState.

title: Daemon: Implement check_health RPC
description: Add logic to daemon/main.go to perform structural graph validation (cycles, inverted epics, orphaned tasks) and return diagnostic results.
close_reason: Implemented check_health RPC in Go daemon with inverted hierarchy and dangling ref checks.

title: Epic: Project Health Diagnostics Engine
description: Implement a comprehensive health checking system to detect common beads project issues like cyclic dependencies, inverted hierarchies, and dangling references.

title: UI: Connection Mode in Project Settings
description: Add a read-only 'Connection Mode' field to the Project Settings screen to report whether the project is in Server or Embedded mode.
close_reason: Implemented connection mode reporting and UI warnings.

close_reason: Implemented connection mode reporting and UI warnings.

title: Capture Database Connection Mode in AppState
description: Update BeadsService to parse the 'mode' parameter from the 'boot_status' RPC notification and expose it via AppState.
close_reason: Implemented connection mode reporting and UI warnings.

title: Epic: Database Connection Safety & Monitoring
description: Track tasks related to monitoring and reporting the Dolt database connection mode (Server vs. Embedded) to prevent lock contentions and data corruption.

title: Fix Settings Screen focus loss during typing
Investigate and Improve Project Refresh Mechanism
Fix Ghostty terminal handoff crashing and command format
description: Pass arguments to Ghostty's -e flag as separate list items instead of a single string, and use the absolute tmux path. Investigate why Ghostty crashed.
close_reason: Passed arguments individually to ghostty -e to prevent it from treating the entire command string as a single executable name

title: Update documentation with tmux dependency
description: Add tmux to the Prerequisites section in README.md.
close_reason: Added tmux to README.md Prerequisites section

title: Add helpful error message when tmux is not installed
description: Catch ProcessException in TmuxService and instruct the user to install tmux.

title: Use internal RPC daemon for assessGraph export
description: Replace direct Process.run('bd export') with an RPC call to the internal beads_service to avoid PATH/binary resolution issues.

title: Investigate App Sandbox for Terminal Orchestration
description: Determine if tmux orchestration allows us to re-enable the macOS App Sandbox.
close_reason: Investigation complete: Sandboxing is fundamentally incompatible with Watcher's AI terminal orchestration which shells out to tmux, bd, and arbitrary terminal emulators. Sandboxing must remain disabled.

title: Fix ProcessException for tmux commands
description: Ensure PATH environment is correctly passed to all Process.run calls in TmuxService.
close_reason: Implemented _getTmuxPath helper to resolve absolute path of tmux binary

description: Migrate the health assessment generation to the new tmux orchestration pattern.

title: Implement UI Loading States and File Polling
description: Update the modals to show a 'Check your terminal' state and poll for the .beads/ai_done lockfile to reload.

title: Update README with Terminal Orchestration architecture

close_reason: Updated README to describe Terminal Orchestration

title: Add Terminal and Tmux Configuration UI
description: Add 'Preferred Terminal' to Global Settings and 'Tmux Session Name' to Project Settings.
close_reason: Added Preferred Terminal global setting and Project-level Tmux Session Name with UI and JSON saving support

title: Refactor PlannerService.generatePlan to use tmux
description: Convert synchronous Process.run to a detached tmux send-keys execution using tee and a lockfile.

title: Build TmuxService in Dart
description: Create a utility class to handle creating sessions, checking existence, sending keys, and spawning terminal emulator windows (Ghostty/iTerm2).
close_reason: Created lib/services/tmux_service.dart with session management and terminal app launch handlers

title: Epic: AI Terminal Orchestration (tmux + Ghostty)
description: Shift AI tools to run interactively via tmux sessions and native terminal emulators, improving context retention and visibility.

title: Research tmux for geminicli backgrounding
description: Investigate using tmux to run gemini asynchronously.

title: Add Create Issue toolbar buttons
description: Add the '+' icon to the toolbars of Dashboard, Kanban, and Tree views.
close_reason: Added ToolBarIconButton to Kanban, Dashboard, Tree

title: Research Apple HIG for form patterns
description: Determine whether to use sheets, popovers, or inspector for creation.
close_reason: Investigated Apple HIG and chose MacosSheet

title: Update BeadsService and AppState for issue creation
description: Hook up daemon RPC to the Dart UI models.
close_reason: Added createIssue to BeadsService and AppState

title: Implement CreateIssueModal
description: Build the MacosSheet with form inputs.

title: Epic: Issue Creation UI
description: Allow users to create tasks and epics directly from the Watcher UI.
close_reason: Completed all subtasks for creating issues

title: task
description: Detect and display if a project requires a newer version of bd based on config/metadata
close_reason: Implemented upstream and project-level version monitoring

title: task
description: Show daemon vs CLI versions side-by-side in Settings screen with warning if mismatch

title: task
description: Monitor upstream steveyegge/beads repo for new releases to notify user
close_reason: Implemented upstream and project-level version monitoring

title: task
description: Add get_cli_version in Flutter that runs bd version via shell

title: task
description: Update daemon/main.go to use debug.ReadBuildInfo() to report actual compiled beads module version

title: epic
description: Manage and monitor the versions of the embedded beads daemon, the local bd CLI, and the upstream beads repository.
close_reason: All monitoring and version detection subtasks completed

comments.author: Hussain Chinoy
comments.text: All subtasks have been completed. The Watcher UI now displays daemon, CLI, and upstream versions, along with mismatch warnings.

title: Review upstream Beads updates for Daemon impacts
description: The upstream beads repository has been updated. Review the recent commits, specifically looking for changes to the internal/storage/doltserver/ APIs, JSONL export structures, or graph dependency resolution logic, as these directly impact the watcher-daemon compiled into the macOS app. Update the daemon Go code if any breaking API changes occurred.

title: Fix Theme Responsiveness in Dashboard StatCards
description: Investigate and fix an issue where the StatCards in the Project Dashboard do not dynamically update their MacosDynamicColor resolution when the system switches between Light and Dark mode while the app is running.

title: Fix UI Contrast and Transparency Artifacts in Settings
description: Investigate and resolve two visual artifacts on the Settings screen: 1) The main macOS sidebar erroneously paints a solid background instead of preserving its native visual effect blur when viewing Settings. 2) The manual project reordering DragTarget ListTiles suffer from poor text contrast when selected in Dark Mode due to default Material canvas overlays. Fixes should be isolated to the Watcher codebase.

title: Implement Reordering in Settings Screen
description: Build a ReorderableListView in the ProjectSettingsScreen to allow users to manually drag-and-drop their projects into a custom order. Wire this list to the appState.reorderProjects method. This provides a clean, maintainable workaround to the macos_ui Sidebar type limitations.

title: Resolve Filtered Parent Rendering in Tree View
description: Investigate the best macOS HIG approach for displaying open child tasks when their parent Epic is closed or filtered out. Options include 'promoting' the child to the root level, or rendering a ghosted/dimmed version of the parent to preserve the hierarchy. Implement the chosen pattern in TreeViewScreen and TreeNode widgets.

title: Implement Sidebar Unread Blue Dot Indicators
description: Implement macOS HIG compliant 'Unread' indicators in the Sidebar. 1) Update AppState to track a lastViewedTimestamp per project in SharedPreferences. 2) Update the file watcher to check the lastModified time of .beads/backup/events.jsonl for all projects. 3) If events.jsonl is newer than lastViewedTimestamp, render a small MacosColors.systemBlueColor circle in the trailing slot of the SidebarItem to indicate background agent or sync activity.

title: Dashboard Refinement Epic
description: Track all Apple HIG compliance refactoring for the Project Dashboard, specifically focusing on responsive Bento Box layouts, glanceable metrics, and accessibility.

title: Implement HIG Bento Box Grid Reflow
description: Refactor the ProjectDashboard stat cards from a rigid Row into a responsive Wrap layout. This aligns with the Apple HIG 'Bento Box' dashboard pattern, allowing cards to reflow gracefully when the macOS window is resized. Additionally, implement Semantics wrappers around the PriorityStatCard so VoiceOver reads the metrics cohesively (e.g., '2 Critical, 5 High') rather than as disjointed visual elements.

title: Refactor Federation Modal to Project Settings View
description: In accordance with macOS HIG, document-level/project-level settings should not be presented as a modal sheet, as this obscures the active context. Refactor the Federation configuration out of the FederationModal sheet and into a dedicated 'Project Settings' screen. Add a gear icon to the Project Dashboard toolbar to access this view.

title: Graph-Aware Event Enrichment (Deterministic)
description: Enhance the ActivityTicker to cross-reference the in-memory graph dependency data. When an event closes a task that has outgoing 'blocks' edges, the ticker should explicitly call out that downstream tasks were unblocked. Also translate raw update_issue events into human-readable sentences based on the field changes (e.g. 'claimed task', 'escalated priority').

title: Semantic Activity Ticker Enhancements
description: Upgrade the Activity Ticker from a raw audit log to a semantic, actionable insights feed. This includes translating raw database events into human-readable actions, utilizing graph dependency data to highlight unblocked tasks, and leveraging lightweight LLMs (like gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview) to summarize how tasks were resolved.

title: Include Task Title in Activity Ticker
description: Enhance the ActivityTicker widget on the Dashboard to display the actual title of the issue being modified, rather than just its ID (e.g., 'G. H. Chinoy performed update_issue on watcher-ulj: Investigate Locked Kanban Cards'). This requires cross-referencing the issueId from the interaction event with the current in-memory issues list.

title: Refactor Global Settings to Dedicated Route
description:
1. Delete the SettingsModal widget.
2. Create a new SettingsScreen widget with a dedicated MacosScaffold and ToolBar to properly align with macOS window traffic lights.
3. Register '/settings' in lib/router.dart.
4. Update the Sidebar 'Settings' MacosListTile to navigate to the new route instead of popping a sheet.

title: Add User Identity Configuration
description: Introduce an Identity/Profile setting in the Global Settings modal. This allows the user to specify their actor name (defaulting to their system/git name) so that UI interactions (like drag-and-drop or updating status) are correctly attributed to them in the Activity Ticker, rather than being hardcoded to 'Watcher UI'.

title: Optimize File Watcher for Near Real-Time UI
description: The current Directory.watch implementation aggressively ignores the entire '.dolt/' directory to prevent infinite refresh loops from background SQL server noise. This accidentally causes the UI to miss live agent database commits. Refactor the file watcher in AppState to strictly monitor the '.beads/backup/' directory instead, and lower the debounce timer from 500ms to 100ms for a snappier, near real-time feel when other agents or sync jobs modify the graph.

title: Investigate Empty Activity Ticker
description: The 'Recent Activity' ticker on the Dashboard is not displaying any events. Need to investigate ActivityTicker, BeadsService, and .beads/interactions.jsonl parsing to understand why interactions are not being correctly loaded or rendered.

title: Investigate Locked Kanban Cards
description: Kanban cards in the UI all appear locked and cannot be dragged to change status. Need to review the KanbanCard widget logic (specifically the Draggable conditional and lock icon) to determine why cards are unexpectedly rendering as locked, even for tasks that are not 'in_progress' or explicitly assigned.

title: Fix Modal Overlay Traffic Lights Alignment
description: Ensure the MacosSheet is configured correctly so that it drops down BELOW the title bar traffic lights (close, minimize, maximize) rather than overlapping them and breaking visual alignment.

title: Update Widget Tree Diagram
description: Regenerate the Graphviz widget tree architecture diagrams in docs/ to reflect the recent refactoring, including the new Global Settings modal and Federation dashboard components.

title: Fix Modal Theming
description: Ensure MacosSheet modals like FederationModal, PlannerModal, and AssessmentModal correctly resolve their background colors dynamically according to the system theme (Light/Dark mode) rather than rendering statically dark.

title: Document Daemon Concurrency Architecture
description: Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with insights regarding Dolt's Server vs Embedded mode lock contention and how watcher-daemon mitigates it using preemptive cleanup and graceful IPC error handling.

title: Handle Uninitialized Projects Gracefully
description: Update the Watcher UI and Daemon connection logic to gracefully detect when a selected directory is not a beads project (missing .beads/ folder), rather than attempting to start the daemon and throwing a generic crash error.

title: Global App Settings: Sync Interval
description: Create a global settings panel/modal that allows users to configure the background sync interval for federated projects. The new modal will be accessible from the main App menu or toolbar.

title: Daemon: Implement add_peer and sync_peer RPC Endpoints
description: Expose JSON-RPC methods in watcher-daemon to execute 'add-peer' configuration changes and trigger a 'bd federation pull' or sync operation using the underlying dolt remotes infrastructure. This may require invoking the dolt environment natively.

title: Daemon: Implement get_peers RPC Endpoint
description: Expose a JSON-RPC endpoint (e.g. get_peers) in watcher-daemon that parses .beads/config.yaml or queries 'bd config list' to return the federation.peers dictionary and current sovereignty tier to the Dart UI.

title: UI: Federation Configuration Settings
description: Create a settings modal or pane in the app that allows users to input a Peer Name and Endpoint URL (e.g., gs://bucket/path) to register new remotes. Add an interval timer to AppState for federated projects to auto-sync.

title: UI: Surface Federation Status and Peers
description: Update the Sidebar to show a distinct icon (e.g., a cloud or sync symbol) for federated projects. Add a 'Federation Status' card to the ProjectDashboard showing configured peers and data sovereignty tiers.

title: Implement Federation Hub Syncing
description:
We want to transition `watcher` from reading local file paths to using Beads Federation to pull issue data from various remote Gas Towns (projects). This allows cross-machine roaming and tracking projects that are not checked out locally.

Tasks:
1. Ensure the Google Cloud Storage backend is accessible by the local `bd` or `dolt` execution environment (e.g. `gcloud auth application-default login`).
2. Have `watcher` maintain a registry of remote endpoints (e.g. `gs://generative-bazaar-001-beads/remotes/<project>`).
3. Have `watcher` programmatically execute `bd federation add-peer <project> <endpoint>` for each tracked project inside its own `bd` environment.
4. Create a background sync job or a manual refresh UI element that runs `bd federation pull <project>`.
5. Update the UI data layer to query the newly synced federation data locally from the `watcher` database, rather than dynamically sniffing around the user hard drive for `.beads/dolt/` folders.

title: Document Daemon Architecture
description: Add a section to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md explaining the rationale for using a Go sidecar daemon communicating via stdin/stdout JSON-RPC instead of direct gRPC or CGO embedding.

title: Dart Daemon Client Layer
description: Refactor lib/services/beads_service.dart to spawn and communicate with the Go daemon process instead of shelling out to the `bd` CLI for every individual update/query.

title: Implement JSON-RPC or gRPC Interface
description: Expose the beads.Storage interface methods (GetIssue, CreateIssue, UpdateStatus, UpdatePriority) over a local IPC socket or stdin/stdout JSON-RPC protocol.

title: Scaffold Go Daemon Project
description: Initialize a Go module (e.g. `daemon/`) within the Watcher repo. Import steveyegge/beads and establish a persistent connection to the Dolt database.

title: Standalone Go Daemon Architecture
description: Transition Watcher from shelling out to the bd CLI for every action to a persistent Go RPC daemon that imports beads as a library. This enables shipping Watcher as a true standalone app and eliminates process-spawning latency.

title: Replace Modal Container Constraints
description: Replace fixed width/height containers in MacosSheets with layout builders or flexible constraints

title: Remove Inspector Close Button
description: Remove the internal X button from IssueInspector and rely entirely on a ToolBar toggle for the endSidebar

title: Adopt Semantic Typography
description: Replace hardcoded TextStyles (e.g. fontSize: 16) with MacosTheme.of(context).typography equivalents (headline, footnote, etc) globally

title: Migrate CupertinoColors to MacosColors
description: Replace all usages of CupertinoColors (iOS specific) with semantic MacosColors across the application

title: Full macOS HIG Compliance Refactor
description: Audit and refactor the codebase to strictly adhere to macOS HIG, including colors, typography, modals, and toolbar interactions.

title: Document macOS HIG Outline View guidelines
description: Document the findings regarding Outline Views in macOS HIG inside docs/ARCHITECTURE.md

title: Add color mappings for blocked and deferred statuses
description: Ensure the trailing status badges correctly map 'blocked' and 'deferred' statuses to distinct colors (e.g. Red and Grey) with appropriate SF Symbols

title: Render issue type icons on all tree nodes
description: Show the issue type icon (bug, feature, epic, etc) for all nested items in the tree view to match native macOS Outline View patterns where leading icons are expected.

title: Remove non-standard prefix in Tree View
description: Remove the non-standard '↳' prefix from child nodes and rely entirely on macOS standard indentation and disclosure triangles

title: Scaffold initial test suite
description: Create test/ directory and add unit tests for AppState and Project models

title: Format codebase to standard Dart style
description: Run dart format on the entire codebase to match standard conventions

title: Author dependencies from the UI (blocks/related)
description: Today drag-and-drop only sets a parent (tree_node.dart) and issue creation hardcodes parent-child. Add: an inspector '+ Add dependency' affordance (pick type + target issue), and/or modifier-drag in the tree to create a 'blocks' edge instead of reparenting. Requires a daemon RPC / bd dep add path for non-parent-child links. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: New view: Dependency graph (DAG) visualization
description: Replace the dead GraphNode path (issue.dart:68-85, beads_service getGraph() returns empty wrappers) with a real DAG view as a new screen alongside Tree/Kanban/Dashboard, showing 'blocks' edges that cross the parent-child tree -- the structure read-aloud actually demands. Consider leaning on bd graph / bd dep tree output rather than a from-scratch layout engine. Larger effort; sequence after P1/P2 land. Depends on the IssueDependencies model foundation.

title: Spike: evaluate libghostty embedded terminal vs AppleScript bridge
description: Research replacing the open -na + AppleScript bridge with an embedded libghostty terminal to eliminate AppleScript fragility (injection, racy delays, wrong-window targeting). Assess libghostty C API maturity (pre-1.0), FFI integration cost with Flutter/macOS, and whether the in-app terminal UX is a real goal. Output a recommendation.

title: Add Firebase and Gemini AI Dependencies
Epic: Direct Gemini Integration & Voice Mode
Research logging strategies for ProcessExceptions
description: Research logging strategies for ProcessExceptions. (Postponed to P3: depends on AppState refactoring to introduce decoupled service layer first).

title: Surface Daemon/Dolt Versions in Settings Menu
description: Investigate a method to expose the compiled Beads and Dolt versions from the daemon to the Flutter UI, likely via a new JSON-RPC endpoint (e.g., 'get_version'). Display these version strings in the SettingsScreen to aid in debugging.

title: Refactor macos_ui Sidebar to support reordering
description: Investigate forking or monkey-patching the macos_ui package, or waiting for upstream support, as the current SidebarItem implementation does not accept custom Widget wrappers (like our ReorderableSidebarItem DragTarget) rendering drag-and-drop within the native sidebar structurally impossible without violating Dart type signatures.

title: Refactor Sidebar to use Reorderable List
description: Update the HomeScreen to map over the appState.projects array using the new ReorderableSidebarItem. Wire up the onAccept callback to fire appState.reorderProjects, ensuring the visual state and the saved state stay perfectly in sync.

title: Custom HIG DragTarget Wrapper
description: Create a ReorderableSidebarItem widget that wraps the macos_ui SidebarItem. Implement Flutter DragTarget logic to detect hover states and draw the native macOS blue horizontal insertion line above or below the item when a project is dragged over it.

title: Implement AppState Reordering Logic
description: Create a reorderProjects(int oldIndex, int newIndex) method in AppState. Ensure it safely mutates the projects list in memory and instantly serializes the new ordered string array to SharedPreferences so the custom sort order persists across launches.

title: Implement Activity Timestamp Subtitle in Sidebar
description: Replace the generic folder/cloud icon in the SidebarItem leading slot with a subtle relative timestamp (e.g., '1m' or '2h') indicating the last time an agent or sync job touched the project's events.jsonl. This provides context alongside the unread blue dot without disrupting the list order.

title: Plan Manual Project Reordering
description: Investigate the Apple macOS HIG for manual sidebar reordering patterns (e.g., drag-and-drop within the ListView). Design a state persistence strategy to save custom order arrays to SharedPreferences instead of the default chronological addition order. We decided against dynamic 'Recent Activity' sorting as it disrupts spatial memory.

title: Update Widget Tree Diagram for Bento Layout
description: Regenerate the widget tree diagrams after refactoring the dashboard metrics area to use new custom widget classes and Wrap layouts.

comments.author: watcher-agent
comments.text: Completed the widget tree diagram updates using the material light theme colors and grouping the views (App Root, Shell, Dashboard, Settings, Tree View, Kanban View) for clarity.

title: Add Contextual Icons to Dashboard Metrics
description: Update the SimpleStatCard and PriorityStatCard widgets to accept an optional IconData parameter. Render the corresponding CupertinoIcon next to the card title (e.g. checkmark for Closed, flame for P0) to improve glanceability as recommended by the Apple HIG.

title: AI Summarization of Closed Tasks
description: Implement an async summarization pipeline. 1) Add a Global Setting to configure the Gemini model ID and region (defaulting to a fast lite model). 2) Introduce an automated summarizer that kicks off when a task is closed, utilizing the selected model to summarize how the task was resolved based on git diffs/context, and posts that summary as a comment or metadata field to be displayed in the Activity Ticker.
close_reason: Implemented dynamic AI model management, Vertex AI region support, and automated issue resolution summarization.

comments.author: watcher-agent
comments.text: Ready to be picked up in a future session. The semantic graph-based deterministic activity ticker logic is complete, laying a solid foundation for adding asynchronous LLM summarization.

title: Design Sidebar Notifications and Dynamic Sorting
description:
Investigate and implement macOS HIG-compliant notification patterns for the Sidebar. Ideas:
1. **Unread Badges:** When a background sync or agent task mutates a project's database (detected via events.jsonl), display a small systemBlue circular badge next to the project name if it is not currently selected. This requires AppState to track a 'lastViewedTimestamp' per project.
2. **Dynamic Sorting:** Add a User Preference to sort the Sidebar project list either 'Alphabetically' or 'By Recent Activity'. The 'Recent Activity' sort would bubble projects with new interactions to the top, similar to the macOS Messages or Mail app. 
3. **Activity Previews:** Consider replacing the generic folder/cloud icon with a subtle '1m ago' timestamp when in the 'Recent Activity' sort mode.
close_reason: Implemented dynamic sidebar sorting, unread activity badges, and activity previews with persistent user settings.

comments.author: watcher-agent
comments.text: Phase 1 complete: Implemented the unread blue dot indicators based on the events.jsonl timestamps (tracked in jn9.1). Still remaining: Dynamic sorting and activity previews.

title: Add Retry Mechanism to Error States
description: Update the main views (Kanban, Tree, Dashboard) to display a clear 'Retry' button alongside any connection or database errors, providing the user an active way to recover from temporary locks or circuit breaker trips.

title: Create IPC Architecture Diagram
description: Generate a Graphviz dot diagram demonstrating the process architecture of Watcher: the Dart UI, the Go watcher-daemon, the underlying Dolt SQL server, and the headless gemini-cli assessor.
